text: Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) suggest that instruction tuning—the process of fine-tuning a model on a broad set of tasks—is an important factor for aligning model output to human desires. However, the main bottleneck of instruction tuning is that high-quality human-written instructions for instructing LLMs are often relatively short in supply, due to their limited diversity, the efforts needed to acquire them, and their costly nature. In this paper, we present SELF-INSTRUCT a self-supervised method for improving the functionality of LLMs, while making efficient use of any minimal seed human-written instructions available. Our framework leverages the power of LLMs to generate novel examples given a given set of seed human-written instructions. We then develop an efficient filter to produce diverse samples in a large quantity. We then learn from the filtered samples in a self-supervised manner and repeatedly recycle the loop to generate more samples from the model itself. Experiments show that SELF-INSTRUCT vastly outperforms a vanilla GPT-3 model and is often close to the performance of LLMs that receive extensive private human-annotated instruction datasets. We make our SELF-INSTRUCT generated training data of 52K instructions available, which we believe will also facilitate future works on self-aligned large-scale LLMs.
headline: Fully Human Written
prediction: We believe that this document is fully human-written
